mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly lifting equity valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below 6.0%, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth and consumer spending, driving near-term equity outperformance.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in >50% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 6.0% within a 3-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Positive S&P 500 forward returns materialize only when real GDP growth exceeds industrial production growth by more than 0.5 percentage points, indicating demand outpacing supply constraints and expanding corporate margins.
failure_condition: The interaction term (GDPC1 growth minus INDPRO growth) shows no predictive power for 60-day forward returns (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-inflation environment, rising federal funds rates compress equity multiples by increasing the cost of capital faster than earnings can adjust, depressing forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are not significantly lower when CPIAUCSL exceeds 3.0% annualized and FEDFUNDS rises consecutively for three months.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales volumes reflect immediate consumer demand strength, which translates directly into higher quarterly earnings revisions and positive short-term equity momentum.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no positive correlation with month-over-month RSAFS changes (correlation coefficient < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly industrial production increase exceeding 1.0% signals robust manufacturing capacity utilization, which historically precedes positive equity returns as supply-side confidence boosts corporate guidance.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are flat or negative in >60% of months where INDPRO growth exceeds 1.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained equity outperformance requires the joint expansion of nonfarm payrolls and housing starts, as simultaneous labor and housing market strength validates broad economic resilience and supports long-duration asset valuations.
failure_condition: The combined signal of PAYEMS and HOUST growth fails to predict 60-day forward returns better than a random baseline (Sharpe ratio < 0.2).

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts decline while the federal funds rate remains elevated, credit constraints tighten across consumer and business sectors, leading to negative equity returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns do not decline when HOUST falls for two consecutive months alongside FEDFUNDS above 0.5%.
